features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported strong Q2 2021 results with worldwide sales of $23.3 billion, up 27.1% year-over-year, driven by recovery across all three business segments. Pharmaceutical sales grew 13.6% to $12.6 billion with strong performance from key brands like STELARA, DARZALEX, and TREMFYA. Medical Devices delivered exceptional growth of 57.2% to $7 billion as procedures recovered from COVID-19 disruptions, with 8 of 11 billion-dollar platforms showing growth versus 2019. Consumer Health grew 9.2% to $3.7 billion with strength across multiple categories including skin health and wound care.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year 2021 guidance, increasing adjusted operational sales growth to 9.5-10.5% for the base business and adding $2.5 billion in expected COVID-19 vaccine revenue. They expect continued momentum in the second half despite some COVID-related volatility, with Medical Devices anticipated to maintain acceleration and Pharmaceuticals positioned for their tenth consecutive year of above-market growth. The company emphasized strong innovation pipelines across all segments, with 17 major product launches in the first half and continued investment in R&D at competitive levels.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 variants and surges that could impact procedure volumes and healthcare capacity, particularly in regions like India, Japan, and parts of the U.S. experiencing hospital strain. Competitive pressures persist in key pharmaceutical markets, including biosimilar competition for REMICADE and novel oral competition affecting IMBRUVICA market share. Consumer Health faces anticipated commodity inflation and distribution cost pressures in the second half of the year that could impact margins.
features.key_drivers: Growth was driven by strong market recovery in Medical Devices with successful new product launches and share gains across multiple platforms. Pharmaceutical performance was fueled by continued strength in immunology and oncology portfolios, with DARZALEX achieving 60% subcutaneous adoption and multiple new indications. Consumer Health benefited from market recovery, new product innovation, and successful commercial strategies, while maintaining margin expansion through operational efficiencies and portfolio optimization initiatives.
